methodsAttempted.query: site:southampton.ac.uk University of Southampton generative AI academic integrity assessment policy students
methodsAttempted.notes: Firecrawl search found the official University guidance page on using generative artificial intelligence during studies, a Web Science Institute news item, research policy pages that only linked to the GenAI page, and unrelated policy pages.
Firecrawl search again surfaced the official student GenAI guidance page as the strongest direct source, plus course, event, project, and publication pages that were not used as policy evidence.
Firecrawl search found historical handbook documents and the official research ethics page, but the direct ChatGPT/academic-responsibility evidence remained on the student GenAI guidance page.

The official student GenAI guidance linked to a public University Library GenAI guide, a ServiceNow Student Hub article, and staff SharePoint guidance. The ServiceNow and SharePoint routes required sign-in, so no login bypass was attempted.

artifacts.summary: The crawl found official University of Southampton student-facing GenAI guidance and a linked University Library GenAI guide with direct evidence for permitted study support, academic-responsibility boundaries, module-specific checks, acceptable and unacceptable use examples, Copilot access, and privacy/copyright cautions. Login-gated Student Hub guidance, a WSI news item, and the AI@Southampton education page were documented but not used as claim evidence.

artifacts.verificationNotes: Firecrawl resolved the ServiceNow article to a Microsoft sign-in page for the University tenant. The article content was not accessed and no login bypass was attempted.

artifacts.sourceTitle: New guidance on use of generative AI published in white paper
artifacts.queryUsed: site:southampton.ac.uk University of Southampton generative AI academic integrity assessment policy students
artifacts.verificationNotes: Official University news page about a WSI white paper for the higher education sector. It is a news/research advisory item, not direct University of Southampton student policy or binding guidance.
Official AI@Southampton page mostly describes education and training opportunities and links back to the University's GenAI position page. It was not used as standalone policy evidence.

artifacts.evidenceSnippet: The fetched page was a Microsoft sign-in page stating: Sign in. The University of Southampton will never ask you to reveal your password.
artifacts.notes: The linked Student Hub article may contain useful guidance, but it was behind institutional sign-in during this crawl and was not used for evidence.

artifacts.evidenceSnippet: In a newly published white paper, the Web Science Institute (WSI) have outlined guidance on the use and integration of generative artificial intelligence (GenAI) in the higher education sector.
artifacts.notes: The page reports on a white paper for the higher education sector and is not direct University of Southampton student policy evidence.

artifacts.evidenceSnippet: The University of Southampton offers opportunities to study artificial intelligence at every level - from undergraduate and master's degrees, to PhDs.
artifacts.notes: The page contains AI education and training marketing plus a link to the official GenAI position page, but does not itself establish a narrower student-use rule.

targets.sourceTitle: Using generative artificial intelligence during your studies | University of Southampton
GenAI - Searching and Generative AI (GenAI) - LibGuides@Southampton at University of Southampton Library

stopConditions: Stop after direct official student-facing GenAI policy or guidance pages are captured.
Do not use login-gated Student Hub or SharePoint pages as evidence.
Do not use news, white-paper, course, research-project, or AI initiative pages as University-wide binding policy evidence.
Do not bypass login walls, robots restrictions, WAF, CAPTCHA, or paywalls.

artifacts.errorReason: ServiceNow article redirected to a Microsoft sign-in page for the University tenant; no login bypass attempted.

artifacts.sourceTitle: Using generative artificial intelligence during your studies | University of Southampton
GenAI - Searching and Generative AI (GenAI) - LibGuides@Southampton at University of Southampton Library

artifacts.claimText: University of Southampton guidance says students are allowed to use GenAI tools like ChatGPT to develop learning and support studies, but should never submit work created or part-created by GenAI for assessments they claim as their own.

artifacts.claimText: University of Southampton guidance says submitting work created by someone other than the student, including GenAI, breaks its Academic Responsibility and Conduct rules and the University will take disciplinary action that may result in mark penalties.

artifacts.claimText: University of Southampton guidance lists acceptable GenAI uses such as arranged assistive technology, study-buddy support, clearly referencing GenAI use, and summarising complex ideas or academic texts; it lists copying GenAI output into work, asking GenAI to write or rewrite work, asking GenAI to answer an assessment question, and sharing module tutor materials in a GenAI tool as unacceptable uses.

artifacts.sourceTitle: Using generative artificial intelligence during your studies | University of Southampton
artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: You are allowed to use GenAI tools like ChatGPT to develop your learning and support your studies. However, you should never submit work that has been created or part-created by GenAI for assessments that you claim as your own work.

artifacts.sourceTitle: Using generative artificial intelligence during your studies | University of Southampton
artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: Work submitted as your own should genuinely be your own work. If you submit work that has been created by someone other than yourself (including GenAI), then this breaks our rules on Academic Responsibility and Conduct. We will take disciplinary action that may result in penalties on your marks.

citation.citationTitle: Using generative artificial intelligence during your studies | University of Southampton

artifacts.sourceTitle: Using generative artificial intelligence during your studies | University of Southampton
artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: Always talk to your module tutor to check how to use GenAI to support your studies in their subjects. How far, and in what ways, you can use generative AI will depend on the particular task or learning you are carrying out and what capabilities you are expected to demonstrate. In other cases, you may be asked to complete a declaration statement that describes how you have used generative AI.
artifacts.rightsNote: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

citation.citationTitle: Using generative artificial intelligence during your studies | University of Southampton
citation.sourceRights: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.sourceTitle: Using generative artificial intelligence during your studies | University of Southampton
artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: Acceptable uses include: using GenAI as an assistive technology by arrangement with our Disability and Inclusion team; using GenAI as a study buddy; citing or clearly referencing your use of GenAI; summarising complex ideas or academic texts. Unacceptable uses include: copying and pasting information created by GenAI straight into your work; asking GenAI to write or rewrite your work or part of your work for you; directly asking GenAI to answer an assessment question; sharing the materials and resources from your module tutors in a GenAI tool.
artifacts.rightsNote: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: Privacy: always check the privacy settings to check what data the GenAI tool is collecting. You may find that anything you enter as a prompt is incorporated into the training data, including any personal or confidential data that you provide. Before using a GenAI tool, consider: Privacy... Copyright: Ensure you have the rights to use the content you're inputting.

artifacts.notes: Approved as an agent-reviewed candidate because the claim follows direct official University wording that students are allowed to use GenAI for learning support but should never submit GenAI-created assessment work as their own.
Approved as an agent-reviewed candidate because the claim is narrowly scoped to the official guidance's statement about Academic Responsibility and Conduct and possible mark penalties.
Approved as an agent-reviewed candidate because the claim preserves the page's module-specific framing and uses may/depend language rather than turning it into a University-wide blanket rule.
Approved as an agent-reviewed candidate because acceptable and unacceptable uses are copied from the official student guidance section and are not extended beyond that list.
Approved as an agent-reviewed candidate because the Copilot access statement is directly supported by the official page and is scoped as current-at-retrieval guidance.
Approved as an agent-reviewed candidate because the claim is limited to privacy and copyright cautions on the official University Library GenAI guide.

artifacts.title: University of Southampton AI Policy Evidence Draft
artifacts.summary: Staged evidence for the University of Southampton includes official student-facing GenAI guidance and a linked University Library guide covering permitted learning support, academic-responsibility boundaries for assessment submissions, module-specific GenAI checks and possible declarations, acceptable and unacceptable use examples, current Copilot access, and privacy/copyright cautions.
limitations: This tracker is not legal advice, not academic integrity advice, and not an official university statement unless a linked source is the university's own official page.
The ServiceNow Student Hub guidance linked from the official page required institutional sign-in during this crawl and was not accessed.

The WSI white-paper news item and AI@Southampton education page were official sources but were not used as policy evidence because they were news, research, training, or initiative pages rather than direct student-use policy guidance.
The staged claims are candidates only; no claim is marked human_reviewed, canonical, or publication-ready.
artifacts.sourceRightsPolicy: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.
